essence: David realizes the SupportSignal data files have drifted from the design document and directs a comprehensive pass over all data documents followed by a generator rerun to realign outputs.

actions.text: Do a comprehensive pass over all related data documents and rerun the generator so outputs match the latest spec

essence: Two documentation fixes for the SupportSignal design doc: expand the RBAC acronym near its usage, and explicitly note the application is designed to be agent-based-system friendly given the shift to agent-based workflows and agent discovery.

actions.text: List definitions for RBAC and other acronyms near where they appear
Note that the application is designed to be friendly to agent-based systems wherever possible

essence: Decision that SupportSignal single sign-on, authentication, and impersonation must use up-to-date off-the-shelf libraries for the chosen stack (Postgres/Supabase/React) rather than rolled-own solutions as done in the past, and this must be woven into the requirements document.

actions.text: Add authentication/impersonation guidance (off-the-shelf libraries, no roll-your-own) into the requirements document wherever relevant

essence: At 50% context David directs a clean ralphy campaign handover: enumerate remaining open items, state exactly which absolute paths/brain/skill were updated, confirm brain updates propagated into the plugin via the renderer, and document everything so a new campaign can launch in a fresh conversation.

actions.text: Document open items, updated paths (brain vs skill), and renderer propagation so a new campaign can start in a separate conversation

essence: UX bugs in SupportSignal's severity classification view (hover tooltips missing, inconsistent small popup on the green status section) get held for round eight while round seven is committed/merged with worktree cleanup, and David flags that done-vs-not-done closure is unclear — possibly a ralphy process problem.

actions.text: Commit round seven, merge the code, and clean up worktrees
Hold new hover/popup UX requirements for round eight and extend requirements from the temp file and conversation notes
Review requirements items 15, 16, 17 from a UX perspective

essence: UX critique of the AWB Workflow Data Panel: the new pop-out panel is too small versus the old full-screen key-value view, flat/nested modes are good but need more view types, expand/collapse states don't persist, configurability is missing, and non-required panels need an optional/skip mechanism backed by a required-flag in the data model.

actions.text: Write up the required workflow data panel and configurability changes (optionally as a TMP file) for developer handoff

essence: Structured gap analysis of the old Agent Workflow UI (strong UX: chevrons, sections, interpolated prompts) versus the new YouTube Launch Optimizer UI (powerful engine: parallel agents, conditional logic, DOM-based workflow, but weak ergonomics), with the conclusion the new UI needs both debug and end-user rendering modes atop the common DOM engine.

actions.text: Produce a UI gap-analysis report comparing the old agent workflow UI with the YouTube Launch Optimizer, including JSON vs markdown/HTML AI-response formatting
Investigate whether Hexfield and Leonardo AI can be driven via a skill, MCP server, or true Chrome-extension shim
Upload the assessment document to a temp folder for the Ralph loop to process

essence: SupportSignal authorization stays role-based with multi-role composition per user and tenant data filters but no role inheritance; a hybrid record flag lets core entities be JSON-managed during transition then DB-only; API-first company onboarding is restricted to internal support staff (Ronnie, David, Angela); Moments That Matter UI is out of scope and the incident runtime will be a document object model rather than POEM itself.

API-first onboarding strategy for SupportSignal: progressively onboard company then sites then participants via JSON/JSONL documents kept in the GitHub repo and pushed through APIs with Claude Code skills/MCP/agents, ship view-only screens first with support-level vs company-level API keys, and defer CRUD data-entry UX to stretch goals except where entry is critical (participants, incidents, moments).

SupportSignal configuration model settled: config is a YAML workflow + Handlebars prompt + JSON input/output schemas merged into an n8n-like inferred DOM with UI hints, with SupportSignal owning workflows/structures while creation/configuration lives in POEM; severity stays prompt-engineered (inherently nondeterministic despite predicate-based structured determinism), 'notification of high severity' is reframed as in-system highlighting, and bulk company/participant CSV upload with validation is proposed.

actions.text: Reframe 'notification of high severity' as in-system highlighting since no notifier exists
Add bulk company and participant upload via validated CSV/spreadsheet paste

essence: Major PO handover: SupportSignal can no longer use Convex after six months of building and must rebuild in two weeks via ordered planning (sign-in, basic roles, data management first, possibly syncing from Convex as a migration bridge), while fixing incident-wizard data persistence bugs, defining Moments That Matter (speech-to-text notes grouped by day/shift, future OMI inject point), and requiring database export as a business-critical feed into POEM for Angela's workflows.

actions.text: Fix data not persisting in incident wizard steps one and two, including session data keys not updating
Plan the two-week Convex-free rebuild ordered by sign-in, basic roles, and data management, with a Convex data-sync migration section
Define Moments That Matter menu/data-entry (time-based notes grouped by day or shift, owned by the support worker, speech-to-text via Groq)
Specify export capability (API or UI) for incidents, participants, moments, workers, sites, companies to feed POEM
Provide a simple what/why/where overview of all outstanding issues and restart round six tracking
